annotations.label: Covariance (informal)

anchor.snippet: covariance is a measure of the joint variability of two random variables

annotations.note: `ProbabilityTheory.covariance X Y μ = ∫ ω, (X ω - μ[X]) * (Y ω - μ[Y]) ∂μ` directly captures the informal notion.

annotations.label: Covariance of two real random variables

anchor.snippet: the covariance is defined as the expected value (or mean) of the product of their deviations from their individual expected values

annotations.note: Defined as `∫ ω, (X ω - μ[X]) * (Y ω - μ[Y]) ∂μ`, exactly matching the deviation-product definition.

annotations.label: Product-form identity for covariance

anchor.snippet: this can be simplified to the expected value of their product minus the product of their expected values

annotations.note: `covariance_eq_sub` gives `cov[X,Y;μ] = μ[X*Y] - μ[X]*μ[Y]` assuming `IsProbabilityMeasure μ` and `MemLp _ 2 μ`.

annotations.label: Covariance of complex random variables

anchor.snippet: The covariance between two complex random variables

annotations.note: `ProbabilityTheory.covariance` is restricted to ℝ-valued random variables; no complex-valued analogue with conjugation found.

annotations.label: Pseudo-covariance

anchor.snippet: A related pseudo-covariance can also be defined.

annotations.note: No definition of pseudo-covariance (E[(Z-EZ)(W-EW)] without conjugation) appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covariance for equally-probable discrete pairs

anchor.snippet: with equal probabilities

annotations.note: The general `covariance` integrates against any measure; the equiprobable finite-sample case follows by instantiation but no dedicated lemma was found.

annotations.label: Mean-free expression for discrete covariance

anchor.snippet: It can also be equivalently expressed, without directly referring to the means

annotations.note: The pairwise-difference identity `(1/n²)∑ᵢ∑ⱼ ½(xᵢ-xⱼ)(yᵢ-yⱼ)` was not found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covariance for unequally-probable discrete pairs

anchor.snippet: but with possibly unequal probabilities

annotations.note: Subsumed by the general `covariance` against a weighted (e.g. finite/discrete) measure, but no dedicated discrete-formula lemma exists.

annotations.label: Covariance via joint probability matrix

anchor.snippet: the covariance is calculated using a double summation over the indices of the matrix

annotations.note: No formula expressing `cov` as a double sum `∑ᵢ∑ⱼ pᵢⱼ (xᵢ-μₓ)(yⱼ-μᵧ)` was found.

annotations.label: Covariance of linear combinations of independent variables

anchor.snippet: Consider three independent random variables

annotations.note: The bilinearity lemmas (`covariance_add_left/right`, `covariance_smul_left/right`) plus `IndepFun.covariance_eq_zero` let one derive the example, but the specific worked example is not stated.

annotations.label: Covariance from joint pmf

anchor.snippet: Suppose that

annotations.note: No worked example computing covariance from a tabulated joint pmf appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Variance as covariance with itself

anchor.snippet: The variance is a special case of the covariance in which the two variables are identical

annotations.note: `covariance_self` proves `cov[X, X; μ] = Var[X; μ]` for AEMeasurable `X`.

annotations.label: Covariance of linear combinations

anchor.snippet: the following facts are a consequence of the definition of covariance

annotations.note: Bilinearity is given by `covariance_add_left/right`, `covariance_smul_left/right`, `covariance_const_left/right`, `covariance_comm`.

annotations.label: Covariance of weighted sums

anchor.snippet: For a sequence

annotations.note: `covariance_sum_sum'` expands `cov[∑ᵢ Xᵢ, ∑ⱼ Yⱼ; μ] = ∑ᵢ∑ⱼ cov[Xᵢ, Yⱼ; μ]`; combined with `covariance_const_mul_*` this covers weighted sums.

annotations.label: Hoeffding's covariance identity

anchor.snippet: A useful identity to compute the covariance between two random variables

annotations.note: Mathlib has Hoeffding's inequality/lemma for sub-Gaussian variables but not the covariance identity `cov(X,Y) = ∫∫(F(x,y) - F_X(x)F_Y(y)) dx dy`.

annotations.label: Uncorrelated random variables

anchor.snippet: Random variables whose covariance is zero are called uncorrelated

annotations.note: No named `Uncorrelated` predicate exists; the property `cov[X,Y;μ] = 0` is used directly throughout.

annotations.label: Independence implies zero covariance

anchor.snippet: are independent random variables , then their covariance is zero

annotations.note: `IndepFun.covariance_eq_zero` shows independent `X,Y` with `MemLp _ 2 μ` have `cov[X, Y; μ] = 0`.

annotations.label: Uncorrelated but not independent

anchor.snippet: The converse, however, is not generally true.

annotations.note: No counterexample (e.g., `Y = X²` with symmetric `X`) demonstrating uncorrelated-but-dependent is formalized.

annotations.label: Uncorrelatedness implies independence for jointly normal

anchor.snippet: if two variables are jointly normally distributed

annotations.note: `HasGaussianLaw.iIndepFun_of_covariance_eq_zero` proves jointly Gaussian with pairwise zero covariance implies independence (generalises the 2-variable Wikipedia statement).

annotations.label: Positively and negatively correlated

anchor.snippet: whose covariance is positive are called positively correlated

annotations.note: No named predicates for positive/negative correlation; sign of `cov` is used directly.

annotations.label: Inner-product-like properties of covariance

anchor.snippet: it satisfies similar properties to those of an inner product

annotations.note: Bilinearity, symmetry (`covariance_comm`) and positive-semidefiniteness (`isPosSemidef_covarianceBilin`) are all established.

annotations.label: Covariance as L2 inner product on quotient space

anchor.snippet: these properties imply that the covariance defines an inner product over the quotient vector space

annotations.note: Mathlib does not construct the inner-product structure on the quotient of finite-variance variables modulo a.s.-constant differences.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality for covariance

anchor.snippet: for random variables with finite variance, the inequality

annotations.note: No explicit `|cov[X,Y;μ]|² ≤ Var[X;μ] * Var[Y;μ]` lemma found; the positive-semidefinite bilinear form is available but the inequality is not stated.

annotations.label: Sample covariance matrix

anchor.snippet: The sample covariances among

annotations.note: No sample-covariance estimator (using `1/(N-1)` Bessel-corrected sums) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unbiasedness of sample mean and sample covariance

anchor.snippet: The sample mean and the sample covariance matrix are unbiased estimates

annotations.note: Unbiased-estimator framework and unbiasedness of sample mean/covariance is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Auto-covariance matrix of a real random vector

anchor.section: Auto-covariance matrix of real random vectors
anchor.snippet: its auto-covariance matrix (also known as the variance–covariance matrix or simply the covariance matrix )

annotations.note: `covarianceBilin` and `covarianceOperator` capture the covariance as a bilinear form/operator on Hilbert space, generalising the matrix; no `Matrix`-valued covariance is defined.

annotations.label: Covariance matrix under linear transformation

anchor.section: Auto-covariance matrix of real random vectors
anchor.snippet: The covariance matrix of the matrix-vector product A X is

annotations.note: `covarianceBilin_map` shows `covarianceBilin (μ.map L) u v = covarianceBilin μ (L.adjoint u) (L.adjoint v)`, the operator analogue of `A Σ Aᵀ`.

annotations.label: Cross-covariance matrix of real random vectors

anchor.section: Cross-covariance matrix of real random vectors
anchor.snippet: cross-covariance matrix is equal to

annotations.note: No cross-covariance matrix between two random vectors is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cross-covariance sesquilinear form on Hilbert spaces

anchor.section: Cross-covariance sesquilinear form of random vectors in a real or complex Hilbert space
anchor.snippet: the covariance of

annotations.note: Mathlib has `covarianceBilin` for a single ℝ-Hilbert-space measure but no cross-covariance form between two distinct random vectors, and no complex/sesquilinear version.

annotations.label: Pearson product-moment correlation coefficient

anchor.snippet: The Pearson product-moment correlation coefficient between two random variables

annotations.note: Grep for `Pearson`/`correlation`/`correlationCoeff` in `Mathlib/` returned no results; the normalized coefficient `cov/(σ_X σ_Y)` is not defined.
